item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something about the over-the-top silliness of setting up an entire heavy machine gun in your living room to shoot at the TV has me loving this article, almost to the exclusion of the rest of its content. I don't really know if I like the thing with the dog bothering you outside the game very much - it feels kind of forced, I guess - or the test log with the D-class committing suicide after the game appeared unbeatable, which seems dubiously tasteful and not really necessary. Still, though, I'm giggling as I sit here and imagine buying this thing at a store, or through mail order, or whatever - "here you go! here's your shitty game cart, shitty manual, and 58-kilogram heavy machine gun! you know how to set it up, right?" And then there's the absolute ruckus that firing the gun will cause, the pile of spent casings (er, do belt-fed weapons eject spent casings? I don't know much about guns) and the inevitable disaster that occurs the first time someone misses the screen during a heated round of play and the rounds fail to demanifest.

I think any sounds the game actually produces must be pretty much irrelevant, because you'll never hear them over the 500 rounds per minute of unmitigated pandemonium blasting off in a confined space. The game really ought to come with earplugs.

I sort of wonder if it might be a better idea to have the thing be loaded with an infinite supply of blank cartridges? That would take away some of the danger factor and replace it with the sort of "arcane frustration" silliness that we saw with SCP-1441 (the cold-fusion paper towel dispenser). A gun loaded with infinite ammo seems to be mostly considered a cliché to avoid around here, but a gun loaded with infinite blanks would be a cute subversion of that, while still keeping most of the over-the-top aspect of having a full-size machine gun to operate a video game.
(Also - while I still think it would be best to remove that part - loading the gun with blanks would not have prevented the subject in the test log from killing themselves; blanks are still quite deadly at point-blank range.)

In any case, you get a mild +1 from me for the pure, absurd situational humor of this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: honestly, this has more in common with smiledog than Duck Season. still, it's simple, it's spooky, and that Game Over image woulda terrified me in middle school.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
mmmmmmm ehhhhhhhhh

the game over image is appropriately TotleighSoft shitty and also creepy as hell, and this does seem like the kind of thing TotleighSoft would make, but like others have said this feels a little too similar to Duck Season except it's anomalous. Making the dog antagonistic isn't particularly grabbing me anyway.

the writing doesn't particularly capture me either, which would have made this a no-vote otherwise

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh the old school creepypasta "this video game is /cursed/". One day the Foundation will find all theses "Lavender Town ghosts will kill you" creepypastas along with the famous haunted black pokemon cartridge.

That said, Addendum 2 is by far the weakest link here. It just felt needlessly gorey and nothing interesting came out of it other than the usual "the playr was so sad he sucieded!" creepypastas have.

I don't know, I'm torn between upvoting for the image, but this really needs a change in tone

-1 for now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Description: SCP-4962 is an anomalous video game titled dack hunt REMIST ER [sic], produced by the TotleighSoft corporation for the Nintendo Entertainment System.

Other SCPs made by / involving TotleighSoft include SCP-2219, SCP-2527, SCP-2803, SCP-3492 and SCP-4587.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I enjoy the style, it felt too much like a combination of both a creepypasta and the game "Duck Season"
No-vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it, a lot

anyway I think you can do more about that, like asking who made this game and did they made other games too.

you can make a series of SCPs about this game maker with SCPs video games.

there are a potential here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is too similar to Duck Season for me to feel anything special about this. it just feels like it was taken and had its grammar funny'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a zany twist on a famous video game, but it's also a cursed video game (complete with requisite hyperrealistic eyes), plus a bit of wacky totally-human speak, and the big capper is a D-class who crazy-to-death's himself. Feels like two conflicting tones in this one, and the tropes used don't really mesh or feel novel to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
me: i like scps about video games i will enjoy myself this will be a treat for me

scp-4962: (game over image)

me: :(

item.title: homk homk
item.content:encoded:
A very old draft that Jack Ike lovingly updated and spruced up, with my assistance. Hope you enjoy!

Asset Sources for Cartridge:

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:M2_Browning,_Mus%C3%A9e_de_l%27Arm%C3%A9e.jpg

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:NES-Cartridge.jpg

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Flying_duck.BDT_6002.jpg

Game Over screen I made myself.
